##NAMES=Anapaite
##RRUFFID=R050510

##LOCALITY=Prats-Sampsor, Spain
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=University of Arizona Mineral Museum
##DESCRIPTION=Pale green tabular crystals lining fractured rock. Probe photo shows two phases. This sample is the lighter phase in the probe photo.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R050510
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Ca_2.00_(Fe^2+^_0.93_Mn_0.07_)(P_1.00_O_4_)_2_·4H_2_O ; (lighter phase)
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder includes light and dark phases. Cell parameters from single crystal
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 6.442 b: 6.806 c: 5.895 alpha: 101.65 beta: 104.27 gamma: 70.77 volume: 234.45 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
